what_it_does: Synthesizes the output of two full-directory coverage rounds over the guided-review-surface, maps the findings against current main, verifies two architectural claims, enumerates the genuinely net-new remainder, records a framing tension, and surfaces explicit decisions for Tim along with a grown build sequence.
observations: Declares the guided-review-surface is the Company's single human-interaction organ (the RHM Walkthrough & Review Organ) and that build-review is merely its first consumer.
Treats the vault organ criteria and the GRS criteria as a union, not a congruence, listing load-bearing criteria each side uniquely contributes.
Claims most of the agent's IAS Phase 0 prerequisites are already on main, with only the mockup:// scheme and an end-to-end FORM-gate render trace still genuinely needed.
Identifies a conflict with the runtime companion's proposal to add a non-turn activation context and a guided_review role, arguing the walkthrough engine and per-turn context already exist.
Lists seven organ consumers, six deferred post-instance-1, and insists instance-1-only mechanisms must not bleed into the shared organ.
Introduces new Round-2 items: cast_posture config axis, screen_reader role, mockup:// scheme, coherence oracle, verify-by-use gate, and a pre-commit FORM-gate hook.
Provides a staged build sequence split into an outer-circuit vault lane and an inner FE/cognition lane, plus enrichment/quality items gated by Tim.
Uses an evidence convention of Observed(file:line), Inferred, and Verified, and repeatedly references Completion Criteria.md group codes.
standouts: The organ-realization claim: the surface is a general RHM organ, not build-review's UI.
The counter-claim that IAS Phase 0 is over-inferred and most prerequisites already exist on main.
The explicit rejection of a new non-turn activation context for the reactive guided dialogue.
The small, enumerated net-new remainder rather than a large rebuild.
The D-I through D-V decisions framed as Tim-owned choices, not foregone conclusions.
conventions: Follows an evidence convention distinguishing Observed, Inferred, and Verified.
Heavy use of criterion group codes (J1, K1, M3, etc.) and cross-references to Completion Criteria.md.
Uses tables to compare scopes, claims, and subsystem states.
Labels recommendations as recommendations and marks genuine Tim decisions explicitly.
Uses status emojis (🟡, 🔴) and build-lane metaphors (outer circuit, inner lane).
concerns: Dense internal jargon and cross-references make the file hard to interpret without the criteria document.
Some items are marked Verified but the verification artifacts are not shown inside this file.
It characterizes another agent's synthesis as stale/over-reached without presenting the other document's content.
The build sequence mixes prerequisites, decisions, and parallel lanes without fully spelling out file-level dependencies.
notes: A meta-synthesis that argues the desired organ architecture is mostly already built and the real remaining work is small, while cataloguing the decisions Tim still needs to make.
questions: What exactly do Completion Criteria.md Groups J–N contain and how do they map to specific code changes?
What is the provenance of the Observed(file:line) evidence and who recorded it?
How were the F0 modularity and singular-wire verifications performed?
What is the current status and content of DESIGN-CORPUS-INTEGRATION.md relative to this map?
What are the concrete S1–S7 acceptance scenarios referenced as the final green condition?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it synthesizes coverage-round findings, verifies claims, names net-new work, and surfaces decisions.
apparent_themes: Evidence-grounded scope control and resistance to over-inference.
Union of criteria rather than declaring one source canonical.
Configurability of cognition posture (lean vs enriched).
Separating forward decisions from current build prerequisites.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to prevent a premature, heavy Phase 0 rebuild by demonstrating that the target architecture largely already exists on main, while honestly isolating the small net-new remainder and the decisions Tim still must make.
novelty: It acts as a self-aware synthesis that cross-checks another agent's dependency claims against current main and reframes a proposed new activation context as unnecessary, which is unusual for a planning document.
contribution: This file contributes a unification map that merges two criteria sets, isolates the genuinely net-new build items, flags over-inferred prerequisites, records unresolved Tim decisions, and proposes a build sequence—serving as a scope-control and decision-support artifact for the guided-review-surface work.
summary_for_embedding: UNIFICATION-MAP.md is a coverage-round synthesis that argues the guided-review-surface is a general RHM organ with build-review as its first consumer, verifies the claim against main, lists a small net-new remainder, rejects a heavy Phase 0 prerequisite, and surfaces Tim decisions plus a grown build sequence.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to ground the convergent organ thesis in observed code, control scope by distinguishing already-built substrate from net-new work, and route remaining decisions to Tim.
